author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Pali Canon - Karaniya Metta Sutta
content:
Sangharakshita in Seminar

The Karaniya Metta Sutta Seminar

(Second Transcriptions Edition)

Contents

Numbers within square brackets [ ] in the seminar which follows refer to the original page numbering of the first edition. This second edition has been completely revised and reformatted so the page numbering has now changed.



Pali Version of the Text taken from the Sutta Nipata
Translation by Sangharakshita written in 1949
Introduction
Metta
Definition of skilful emotion; loba-dosa-moha

VERSE I:
Karaniyam... abhisameca
Attha - Ultimate aim or good
Kusala - Practical intelligent skilfulness
Yam tam....abhisamecca
Santan padan - State of peace
Peace and dukkha
Abhisamecca - Proficient in
Buddha's teaching: conditional or imperative
Christian attitude to other religions
Christ - a Bodhisattva?
Separating fact from myth
Sakko ... anatimani
Sakkyo - Capable and vigorous
Uju - Upright, straightforward
Suju
Suvacha - Well spokenc
Asa - Amenability
Mudu - soft, tender, flexibility
Discussion on implications of these attributes

VERSE II:
Santussako - contentment
Subharo - easily supportable
Demarcation between monk and laity?
Work and a spiritual life
Appakicco - with few duties
Sallahukavutti - of light wants
Non-spiritual activity
Santindriyo - with pacified senses
Nipaka - not presumptuous/prudent
Appagabbo - modesty

VERSE III:
He should not commit ... censure him
Other wise men - rules and spiritual community
Vinnu - those who know


VERSE IV:
May all beings.....wholesome
Sukhi - happy, wholesome
Khemino - secure
Sukhitatta - may they be of happy self
Friendliness and friendship

VERSE V 42
Whatever living beings .... happy minded.
Let none......harm another
Deceit
Patigha - malice
Byarosana - hatred
The difference between anger and hatred
Recapitulation
How far can metta go?
The practice of developing metta by stages
Erotic feelings in the metta bhavana
Clear recognition of feelings
Erotic and sexual
Vedas and system of Indian philosophy
Modern Hindus and worship of the Buddha

VERSE VII:
Just as.....towards all beings
The analogy of maternal love to metta
Risking her own life - objectivity
Recapitulation
Integration of emotions; directing ‘negative’ emotions towards the Buddha

VERSE VIII:
Let his thoughts......without any enmity
Asapatam - without any enemy
Universality of metta and brahma viharas
Exclusivity of positive emotion? devotion
Innate expansiveness of positive emotion
Feelings associated with Christianity

VERSE IX:
Whether he stands......develop this mindfulness
Highest conduct
Brahman - sublime
Adhittheyya - exercise

VERSE X:
Dassanana sampanno - perfect vision
Absolutising conceptual constructions
The fruit of practice - rebirth
Translations of the Sutta
Regular and Irregular steps
*******
